Hi guys, this is Mark McCray and I can see some of our regulars here. Hi David, Hi Jeff, Hi John, Michael, Rich. So today we have Bond and Kevin Halbert who are with us and our guests today are Keith Kranz and Ralph Burns. And just a little bit of history of how I met Keith and Ralph is that over the years I have hired and worked with a lot of people on Facebook to help me with my Facebook marketing and I never really got any good results. And so I've always had a kind of lazy eye looking for somebody or a company or anyone who could really help lift my Facebook marketing. And Keith and Ralph kept popping up where they are sort of the cutting edge in Facebook marketing right now I think and a lot of people seem to agree with me. So I'm going to introduce Keith and Ralph right now but another thing that I just want to mention their website is Dominate Web Media and they also have a book on Amazon and the Amazon book is on my screen just now. It's the ultimate guide to Facebook advertising. And so welcome Keith and Ralph. It's great to be here. Great to be here, thanks for having us. So our kind of format today will be that Keith and Ralph will give us some share, some of the information that they know about Facebook and then we will have myself, Bond and Kevin ask a few questions and then any questions that they are from the audience so feel free to write in the chat and we can ask Keith and Ralph at the end of it. And one thing I wanted to ask you both was how did you get started and how did you join up? Oh how did we get started in the world of Facebook advertising and how did Ralph and I kind of join up? Yeah. Yeah good question. So long story short I actually went to college to become an airline pilot so I kind of did that whole route and ended up getting a job and flying for Horizon Air, part of Alaska Air for about six years so I've got about 4,000 hours and through that process my entrepreneurial spirit started kicking in and I ended up getting into some real estate back in the early 2000s and kind of leveraging some of that stuff into local business and I ended up owning a part of two different franchises basically between the period of a couple years four to six different locations and in about 2008 or so 2009 I started like digging into this whole online marketing world and I really started going to the local GKIC, Dan Kennedy out here in Glaser Kennedy Insider Circle so I started learning direct response marketing right and then I started just falling in love with this internet world and so long story short from there I as I was learning this direct response marketing I started seeing a couple Facebook ads and I'm like wait a second here. We would like there's a few times where we ran we spent $10,000 a month on a billboard on a not so busy road and with really not very good tracking in place or anything we're doing direct mail like BioPack direct oh not great direct mail back then but you know the typical co-op local TV radio stuff and all of a sudden where we could put up the billboard but instead of paying that $10,000 we actually put it up for free and then just pay like every time a car would pull over that's in our exact ideal audience it's like a digital billboard it would only display to like our exact ideal car and then we wouldn't pay for that until they pulled over and call the number on the billboard or you know went to the website and that's kind of when I went wow this is crazy so kind of went all in on Facebook that and long story you know basically a few years later I connect with Perry Marshall and through a client that we were working with and then started doing stuff with Perry Marshall and then he asked me to write the book with him so we ended up doing about three programs together for his audience and co-authored the book and next thing you know Ralph came through one of my programs and Ralph you could probably tell how we can do was kind of organically it's been a pretty cool relationship how it's how it's gone down really well I got on Facebook advertising because I was fired from my job so I had to and a big corporate shay I came from the corporate world on the fortune 500 companies but I started my own blog and after reading the four hour work week and then they found out about that and they figured out a way to get me out of there and I was a director of sales at that point in time so they they downsized me out of a job and but I did know how to do paper click advertising on Google and then migrated into Facebook as an affiliate and that's back when you could basically target you know age, gender and where they lived so after about the fourth or fifth time my wife walking in on me you know doing Christian mingle ads on Facebook I decided I should probably diversify a little bit so I went into a lot of other sort of different types of media and went away from Facebook for a while but then when I started doing huge media buys as an affiliate in the paper click arena and then Facebook changed their whole advertising platform about two years ago and I think that's when I met Keith you know when they finally Facebook finally partnered up with big data which makes it really the most powerful advertising platform in the world in our opinion it's why we focus so much on it and we met at a conference and hit it off and then started working together sort of on an initial basis and and now we're full blown partners and dominate web media so it's been actually driving traffic to a blog post and there's a few different reasons why it's getting such cheap clicks but the main reason why is it gets a high relevant score this is a new thing that Facebook has initiated and it's similar to Google's quality score and it's a big big deal that nobody right now is really understanding telling you right now there's not many people teaching what we're going to teach you guys today and it might feel a little bit like different or over your head a little bit but I promise you if you if you if you pay attention and and put some of this into action it's it will be game changing for you so this post right here goes to a blog post where people do not have to opt in for okay now if there's calls to action on that blog post where people will opt in and that's great and if they don't though it doesn't matter because they're going to get the next day or so they're going to see a lead gen ad go into a webinar but the only people that might see that ad that's going to the webinar are people that are already visited this post and I'm going to show you guys a much more a better example here in a second okay but the point is is that what can happen now is if you can use this kind of we call it goodwill content to warm people up you can get you can take a click cost from like almost three dollars down to 30 cents okay that's 10 times less 10 times less and when you're taking them to that blog post you can track every single visitor that hits that blog and they can be automatically added to your website custom audience okay you have to think about this website custom audience as a list okay it's like an invisible list that Facebook owns but it's inside your Facebook account because now you can run any ad to these people across the board that are not where you have a hot girl like this point is is this ad right here is averaging nine cents per click to website and it's going to this blog post which is 3,300 words so people to take action on a landing page but you also want to write high value educational content that is all this seeding your your opt-in seeding your sales project product all along the way okay you're designing this process this blog post right here it's 3,300 words notice it's got strong calls to action over here on the right side this is a product right here actually this is an opt-in page towards the bottom there's links that go right to her sales page where she's getting a 5% conversion rate from visitors from this page okay um now this right here is just some of the numbers this ad is running right now every day okay it's an evergreen ad because it's just it doesn't it's such so much value if you look back here you see this has got 2,000 likes and it's got 1200 shares okay that's really really important so this what I call I I call this the like to share ratio okay so you have a two to one ratio right so this one is about you know more than that it's like five to two almost right um no it'd be like five to three I guess but the point is is there's over half of the amount of shares as there are likes what that means is people like this content right and if this was going to a to an opt-in page you would have if you had 2000 likes here you might have 200 shares so it'd be like five to one in the ratio and what that means is let's just have the relevant score on this no it doesn't um what that means is this when it has a high like to it has a lot of shares and also when they hit this page they don't bounce right back to Facebook those are like the the two most important things if people leave Facebook and it bounce right back and there's a low share rate then you're going to have a low relevant score you're going to probably have a one or two and what that's going to do it's going to your your click costs are going to go way way up and you're going to lose impressions that's what's happening to people right now that are running um ads to just purely lead you and not doing any of this and then they eventually get their account shut down um so that's kind of the process does she drive traffic to elite to opt-in pages heck yeah she does and opt in to consume it and then this is a fan we run light getting campaign so just in the background maybe ten dollars a day where you're getting new fans every day and um there's a lot of reasons why you do want fans because you can retarget them with ads and then you can also have more social proof more friends of fan connection targeting which is the most powerful part of that but it's it's hard to explain without really showing it in the ads manager but the point is is this is the top of your funnel here fans goodwill content as people move down the next step would be what a lead magnet or a webinar or maybe a coupon or a demo of a software it just whatever your kind of main front end lead is is your next logical step right and then after they opt in they might land on a front end sales page right this might be a 49 dollar product uh 99 free plus shipping offer could be a uh 99 dollar product okay it could be a webinar um some in some cases right but then you have an up then you have maybe the front end kind of order form for that product and then you have your upsells and that's a super basic funnel and I want you to think about this as kind of warm cold it's kind of a lukewarm real world and this is when you kind of maybe send them a proposal here and this is when you send them the actual you know credit card how to pay for and then you have higher ticket stuff so that's is I mean you can still drive traffic directly to a landing page for a lead magnet and capture email addresses you can certainly do it we still have clients that do it and we do it ourselves um but if you really think about this funnel right here and sort of the way that the real world works is that in the offline world you usually don't you know you meet that guy at the party who asks you to do business with him or set the appointment three seconds after you meet him that's almost like asking for a name and an email address on your first interaction and it's it's somewhat unnatural and what facebook is doing is they're sort of dragging advertisers kicking and screaming now a lot of direct response advertisers and you know a lot of the clients that we have our direct response they want leads they want people to capture that email put them into a nurture sequence and then ultimately sell them maybe a low end product and then a high end product and then something maybe on the back end um facebook in the algorithm the way that it works now is that if you try to drive a tremendous amount of traffic just to a lead magnet facebook won't allow you in most cases so it's it's you know dependent upon you know your niche and how competitive it is and so forth and so on but if you're talking about in like the coaching niche and the business niche for example they will not give you the impressions in order to get as many leads as you want unless you actually have a step ahead of that and if you really think about that like that first step is like that ungated content content that you know it's that tip on the 529 plan that you meet you know when the guy gives that to you at the party that you meet and then and then maybe you think about hiring them as your financial planner after that so facebook is really a sort of mirroring the real world and i think they always wanted to do this whenever they started with the ad platform about two years ago really making it much more robust but now advertisers are realizing that they have to adapt to this way of doing it or they're not going to get the impressions or they're going to pay more for leads or they're going to get into situations where their account is getting into you know a banned situation or lots of ads being rejected which we see all the time and what this ungated content this step ahead of the lead magnet does ahead of the lead is it actually warms them up so that they end up buying in the back end more they're a more qualified candidate for you so facebook is dragging everybody to this we always knew it was there we advocated doing it now everyone's forced to do it this way and i think we're seeing this you know with the ad spend that we have with our clients and i think it's just going to become more and more stringent as time goes on so yeah good point ralph okay cool and let me know if you can't see it okay so basically what we want to do is think about remember that funnel that i just showed you so um that funnel that i just showed you think about this row right here as that kind of funnel okay so actually i'm gonna this think about this row here as that funnel so you've got your um the top here is your cool interest based targeting okay and then over here are your fans as you get down as you go as you go down this farther and farther you've got fans and then you have non-converted website and video custom audiences okay these are they're they're not leads right but you have to think about them that way because in facebook when you have a fan you can always target fans only in your ads right and then non-converted website and video custom audiences what that is is anybody that lands on a blog post they now go in that website custom audience at facebook just copy and paste the pixel we have a lot more advanced trainings on how to do this it's very simple actually inside facebook ads university but basically um you can if you get a thousand people to let's say you get a thousand people to a blog post to a blog post which is right here then you're going to have a thousand people on that facebook website custom audience that you can now retarget to your optin page or to your sales page so that's really important to understand and the other thing that facebook has is made so many improvements on lately is video so if i run a facebook video ad now i use the video views objective they're actually improving it now and you can use other objectives but let's say i run a video and i want to run a video ad to a webinar then i do that and i put i maybe i get a hundred registrations well typically with the video and facebook it's going to cost you anywhere between one cent to six cents per view sometimes up to like ten cents per view if it's like a super small kind of audience but typically around one to five or six cents per view is what you're going to pay and you get to now facebook will add all those viewers onto your website onto your a new custom audience so it's a retargeting list basically so now you can not only retarget people that land on a blog post but you can retarget people that watch your video in the news feed even if they don't click through it's super powerful so it's massive branding so we'll run video ads i run video ads all the time to a webinar or i ran video ads when i promoted this uh launch the digital marketer just did and i was cool because i guess i was promoting their product but i was also building my own website custom audiences with all my videos so i was branding myself in a massive way so with with you want to always be thinking about with this mark that this social media stuff because people are not always ready to buy yet right so you're always trying to figure out how you can use direct we're going to be able to target those fans with these ads down here and get a lot cheaper clicks and a lot cheaper leads and we're going to also get a thing that's called connection targeting so if you ever next time you go to facebook take a look at the news feed and i bet you'll see an ad or just even the business facebook page that says um you know ralph burns also likes this uh ralph burns and 47 others also like nike you ever seen that right that's called what that is is social proof and that's called connection targeting that's when i might say i want to target fans of digital marketer that also have a friend that is a fan of dominate web media so you get that connection targeting and it gives you lower click costs higher click through rates uh just higher conversion rates fan right that makes sense yeah no totally makes sense uh what a lookalike audience off a website custom audience is probably in our experience um it's a it's potentially a good list the best lookalike audiences that we use and sometimes we use them without any other interest targeting at all um are from a conversion pixel so if somebody goes to the gary helbert letter and then opts in the more conversions there are on that pixel on the thank you page the better that lookalike audience potentially is so for example keith gave an example of um you know a lookalike audience a friend of ours is using that has 22 000 conversions on it we've got a client that probably has at least that many on there maybe 40 000 it's like the golden pixel for us and that lookalike audience is incredibly powerful so we make iterations of that based off that pixel you know for a variety of different things but um when it comes to lookalike audiences the more data you give facebook on it like for example that website custom audience that you're talking about if it has a hundred people in it it's less powerful than it if it has a hundred thousand people in it by a factor of however many that is thousand um so the more you have there the better potential audience you can actually have and to target for a lead generation for sale whatever it happens to be but sometimes those audiences like you say i mean they're typically in the us they're anywhere between 1.2 and 2.4 million so they're really large so what we'll do is to narrow those down we'll overlay the exact demographic of our ideal customer like if we know that that ideal customer is 35 to 44 in mail we'll overlay that and all of a sudden that'll come down to maybe 400 500 thousand people or maybe we'll know for example like for a coaching client we'll add in an interest of coaching on top of a lookalike audience and now that interest goes from a couple of million down to maybe 400 500 000 those are super targeted potential customers for you so the more you can layer your targeting the better but if your pixel has like for example in that one client that has 30 or 40 000 hits on that pixel we're just using the lookalike audience with age demographics and that's it and it's converting exactly the way that we want unfortunately they're not doing a lot of the stuff that we're talking about here with the content amplification everything else so facebook is restricting the amount of impressions that we're getting but um you're on the right track with what you're doing because those lookalike audiences are going to be really super powerful for you as long as you use them sort of a lot of different ways and test them out okay i have a question which we were all amazed back in the day that you were versions of this image okay and you can test a different image um and and then maybe you find out which image is winning and then you can test different copy now that would be kind of the way to look at it so let's say uh i don't explain it it's not super confusing basically you don't have to have a ton of different campaigns going as you're first starting out but it also depends on the size of your audience right it also depends on the size of your audience right um like the audience the bigger your audience is and the ad has to work its way through that the longer that ad will run before you have to come up with something fresh or something new and then if you're if you are um but you're always going to be looking at it changing your ads and creating something fresh eventually or testing different things so just leave the runner the biggest winner to start working on the medius list keep going at that until is there actually that that leads to a good to a good question is there is there a telltale sign or something tells you that an ad is played out and it's time to move it on before facebook says hey you know this thing got some negative feedback or because people are because because one thing i did see for a while last year some people got really sick and tired of some ads that they didn't mind it's just they got sick of seeing it six times a day and so then they got the negative feedback yeah totally a good good question so not there's not a super it's not super scientific on that however there's a couple of ways one is the frequency is the big one right so you can look at your campaigns and you can see if the frequency which is the amount of times that ad is displayed to individual user starts to start to go up then you might start to you know look at things and if you see the frequency starting to come up and maybe your click-through rate is starting to decrease a little bit um then that's a sign now however you have to realize that let's go back to facebook that there's a huge difference between this newsfeed ad here and these two right column ads here right so this newsfeed ad is pretty much takes up almost the entire newsfeed right so this here this is like this is content and this is native advertising facebook will not display this ad over and over to the same person okay they'll limit it to typically to around two a frequency of two however if you have this ad over here if you want to have a frequency of 30 to like a smaller audience they will let you continue to run that but you can look at you'll start to see your click-through rates go down and your cost per click come up a little bit and so it kind of depends on what your your goal is if you're you know if you're trying to get people to show up on a webinar then you know you want a high frequency kind of but it's more of a but it kind of depends but you can really um start to see inside your reporting um kevin did you have any other questions oh no that's great no it's great bond you good yeah i'm good i actually just wanted to add something which is you know because you know the thing is this is so ingrained in kevin and i you know because you know you talk about how warm the lead is and it's based on their actions and everything and this has always been a numbers business on facebook off facebook and everything but i want to point out one other thing which is in the future it's going to be very lucrative to take a look at your old ads to see what's you know because in newspaper advertising you could run a campaign and then it would start to no longer be run up you know you couldn't be profitable but you let that thing rest for a while then all of a sudden you get hit the papers again so i just want people to remember that you know if you're watching this a year from now take a look at the ads that are working right now because there's a good chance that they might not you know that the audience may have gotten a break and that's what it is really it's just like the relationship marketing you talk about that's a great point yeah that's a great point and that's yeah and that's one reason why um let's say you're going to run uh an ad and you want to let's say you want to split test like three images and sometimes by doing that it actually will help because you're rotating it's it's it's you're rotating your ads at the same time um just just realize that facebook is kind of weird that way if you have more than like two or three ads and inside an ad set and facebook a lot of times will limit the impressions that you're getting they'll kind of just give the one that's getting the highest click-through rate and kind of try to optimize it quote-unquote so um sometimes like you said you might just have to just pause it wait a what's your thoughts on special coupons offers for fans of a particular product the offers do they work well if people have an offer only if there's a real incentive um there's like a discount coupon or some kind of code that gives them something that they ordinarily wouldn't get if they were to buy it in a in a you know through your normal channels so there's gotta be a real either steep discount or something really special associated with it that's when we've seen offers work the best for those types of you know for coupons or discount codes those work really well on retargeting because you're going to give them maybe your full priced offer but then maybe they don't take it but then you now have them pixeled and you can retarget them with another ad that maybe says hey you know i noticed that you know still interested in whatever it happens to be get 20 off now for the next 24 hours or whatever it happens to be and run that ad to that website custom audience because you know they are already interested they just need a little nudge to push them over the edge so i would use it in that way we don't use offers quite as much as we used to but local business if you have a local business like a restaurant a facebook offer will work all day long but um for certain types of like national type of businesses it's going to be something you're going to want to offer up to somebody that's already kind of been into your world unless it's just something that you know it's just so such a great deal people will share the heck out of it so i've seen some great you know
